# Architectural Drawing Description

This is a historical architectural blueprint on aged, cream-colored paper showing detailed construction drawings of a large Victorian-era building. The main composition features a longitudinal section (cross-section) view at the bottom center, depicting a complex multi-story structure with varied rooflines, a prominent tall spire or tower on the left side, and multiple interior levels with detailed structural elements including supports, staircases, and framing. The building shows ornate Victorian architectural details with peaked roofs, vertical ribbing, and decorative elements throughout.

In the upper right corner is a separate detailed section drawing showing the "Staircase-Section" with internal architectural details and measurements. The upper left contains a smaller detailed drawing of what appears to be the tower or spire element.

The document is labeled with various architectural notations and measurements in handwritten text along the bottom, including "Longitudinal Section" and references to different building sections and measurements. Handwritten notes appear in the upper left corner and margins. A color reference bar is visible on the right edge of the paper. The drawing uses brown/tan ink lines and cross-hatching to indicate different materials and structural elements, typical of 19th-century architectural documentation.

# Architectural Floor Plan Description

This is a historical architectural drawing on aged beige paper showing the second floor plan of a building designed by F. McMillous & L. Son, Architects, located at 330 Pine Street. The document shows significant aging with brown staining and marks throughout.

At the top of the page is a color reference scale containing bands of blue, cyan, green, yellow, magenta, pink, and dark gray colors, along with a measurement ruler.

The main floor plan is rendered in brown ink lines with handwritten labels and dimension annotations. The layout depicts multiple rooms arranged across the floor:

**Upper section:** Two large rooms labeled "Bedroom" (each measuring approximately 16'2" x 16'8") separated by a central corridor area with small fixtures marked, and smaller rooms on either side.

**Middle section:** Several rooms labeled "Bed Room," "Bath Room," and a "Hall," with varying dimensions marked throughout.

**Lower section:** A more detailed arrangement of numerous smaller rooms labeled "Bed Room" (numbered 1-8), "Bath Room," and "Hallway," creating what appears to be a multi-unit residential layout.

Diagonal dashed lines throughout indicate wall alignments and structural relationships. Small squares and rectangles represent fixtures like doors and windows. The notation "Scaled Eight Feet to One Inch" appears at the bottom, establishing the drawing's scale.

520__a-1: This is an architectural floor plan on aged, beige parchment paper showing the first floor layout of a multi-story building. At the top of the image is a color reference scale with bands of navy blue, cyan, green, yellow, magenta, pink, white, and dark gray. The plan itself is rendered in brown ink lines and includes detailed room layouts with labeled spaces such as "Old Ladies Dining Room," "Children's Dining Room," "Kitchen," "Pantry," "Hall," "Bed Rooms," "Servants' Room," and various other chambers. Dimensions are noted throughout in feet and inches. A central vertical corridor connects different sections of the building, with stairs indicated in multiple locations. The bottom of the plan contains text reading "SCALE: EIGHT FEET TO ONE INCH" on the left side and "E.M. GALLSALL & SON, ARCHTS." with an address "530 Pine St. S.F." on the right side. The notation "FIRST FLOOR" appears centered at the very bottom. The document shows significant age with staining, discoloration, and creasing visible across the surface.
